This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
CITA records
Centro Independiente de Trabajadores Agricolas
8532
5.5 cubic feet. 5.5 cubic feet.
1991-2012. Reports, photographs, digital media. Reports, Board of Directors records, subject files, audits, photographs, DVDs and CDs documenting CITA. Collection material in English 9765579
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
January 2017
EAD encoding:
Jude Corina, January 2017
ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
CITA, or the Independent Farmworkers Center, was created in 1991, with the help of Rural and Migrant Ministry, as a grassroots farmworker membership organization. Its first office was in Florida, New York and in 2001 it moved to Albion, New York. Its main focus was to educate farmworkers about their rights and to organize workers to negotiate directly with their employers to resolve problems with work or housing.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Reports, Board of Directors records, subject files, audits, photographs, DVDs and CDs documenting CITA.
Cite As:
CITA Records, #8532. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Photographs. Housing, Rural -- United States. Migrant agricultural laborers -- United States. Migrant labor -- United States. Agricultural laborers -- New York State.
